spec_match.rationale: The page now follows the draft's sequence-of-MediaStreams shape and reads primary-screen metadata from ScreenCaptureMediaStreamTrack.screenDetailed(), not from MediaStreamTrack settings.

interactive.rationale: Probe button calls the API, flattens the returned streams into track cards, shows track settings plus screenDetailed() metadata, and the stop button releases every returned stream.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card now promises one stream per display plus real screenDetailed() primary metadata, matching what the page renders.

edge_cases.rationale: The false primary-screen heuristic is gone; remaining useful follow-ups are deviceId stability and track constraint behaviour.

references.rationale: See also links the chromestatus entry and the WICG spec.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, no static-card pattern, badge override has both background and color set.

openQuestions.title: Add a deviceId stability probe
openQuestions.detail: The spec promises stable deviceIds across captures so compliance recorders can re-identify monitors. Add a 'capture twice and compare' button that asserts deviceIds match across two consecutive calls.

openQuestions.title: Show the `applyConstraints` failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Per-screen track-level constraints (e.g. requesting a different displaySurface) usually reject. A short demo of `applyConstraints` succeeding for width/height and failing for displaySurface would teach the affordance.

summary: The per-track inspector now uses the draft's stream sequence return shape and obtains primary-screen status from track.screenDetailed(), eliminating the fabricated index-zero primary badge.
